---
title: "How It Works"
---

# How the tool works

This page describes the **architecture**, **annotation pipeline**, and the role of each database.

## Overview

Orchestration is centralized in `annotation/cli.py`. For each `(gene, organism)` pair, the pipeline:

1. Checks the **local cache** (`.cache/gene_annotations.json`);
2. Queries APIs in a logical order (NCBI → Ensembl → UniProt → derived databases);
3. Assembles a **24-column row** with clickable HTML links;
4. Computes a **coverage report** and exports files.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    subgraph input [Input]
        F[gene,organism]
    end

    subgraph core [Primary sources]
        NCBI[NCBI Entrez]
        ENS[Ensembl REST]
    end

    subgraph derived [Derived sources]
        UP[UniProt]
        PS[ProSite]
        PF[Pfam / InterPro]
        GO[Gene Ontology]
    end

    subgraph independent [Independent sources]
        STR[STRING]
        KEGG[KEGG]
        PDB[PDB / RCSB]
    end

    F --> NCBI
    F --> ENS
    NCBI --> UP
    ENS --> UP
    UP --> PS
    UP --> PF
    UP --> GO
    F --> STR
    NCBI --> KEGG
    F --> PDB

    NCBI --> ROW[Annotated row]
    ENS --> ROW
    UP --> ROW
    PS --> ROW
    PF --> ROW
    GO --> ROW
    STR --> ROW
    KEGG --> ROW
    PDB --> ROW
```

## Python modules

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `annotation/cli.py` | CLI, parallelism, orchestration, HTML export |
| `annotation/databases/ncbi.py` | Entrez queries (Biopython), retries, rate limiting |
| `annotation/databases/ensembl.py` | Ensembl REST API (genes, transcripts, orthologs) |
| `annotation/databases/uniprot.py` | ID mapping (NCBI/Ensembl → UniProt) |
| `annotation/databases/prosite.py` | ProSite motif scanning |
| `annotation/databases/pfam.py` | Domains via InterPro API |
| `annotation/databases/go.py` | Gene Ontology annotations |
| `annotation/databases/string_db.py` | STRING IDs and networks |
| `annotation/databases/kegg.py` | KEGG IDs and pathways |
| `annotation/databases/pdb.py` | Structures via RCSB Search API v2 |
| `annotation/cache.py` | Thread-safe per-gene JSON cache |
| `annotation/export.py` | CSV, Excel, JSON |
| `annotation/summary.py` | Coverage statistics |
| `annotation/paths.py` | Output paths (`results/`) |
| `annotation/config.py` | Global configuration |
| `viewer/app.py` | Flask browsing application |
| `annotation/gui.py` | Tkinter graphical interface |

## Per-gene pipeline

The function `annotate_gene(gene_symbol, organism)` runs these steps:

### 1. NCBI (`ncbi.py`)

- Search gene by symbol and organism;
- Fetch official name, gene IDs, RefSeq RNA/protein transcripts;
- Convert organism name to NCBI taxonomy;
- **Rate limiting**: configurable delay (`NCBI_DELAY`) between Entrez calls.

### 2. Ensembl (`ensembl.py`)

- Look up Ensembl gene ID;
- Links to genome browser and orthologs page;
- Associated transcript and protein IDs;
- Multi-division support (vertebrates, plants, protists, etc.).

### 3. UniProt (`uniprot.py`)

- Map NCBI and/or Ensembl IDs to UniProt accessions;
- Text search fallback if mapping fails;
- Official protein name.

### 4. ProSite and Pfam

- **ProSite**: motif scan on UniProt sequences;
- **Pfam**: protein domains via InterPro API (replaces the legacy Pfam XML API).

### 5. STRING (`String_id_and_network.py`)

- STRING identifier and interaction network link;
- Network image (when available).

### 6. PDB (`PDB.py`)

- 3D structure search via RCSB API v2;
- Links to matching PDB entries.

### 7. KEGG (`kegg.py`)

- KEGG identifier and associated pathways;
- Direct REST API (`rest.kegg.jp`).

### 8. Gene Ontology (`GO.py`)

- GO terms: molecular function, biological process, cellular component;
- Based on UniProt IDs.

## Output table columns

The final table has **24 columns**:

| # | Column | Source |
|---|--------|--------|
| 1 | Gene Symbol and Organism | User input |
| 2–5 | NCBI (name, gene ID, RNA, protein) | NCBI |
| 6–10 | Ensembl (gene ID, browser, transcript, protein, orthologs) | Ensembl |
| 11–12 | UniProt ID and name | UniProt |
| 13–14 | STRING ID and network | STRING |
| 15–16 | ProSite motifs and graphical view | ProSite |
| 17–18 | Pfam domains and graphical view | Pfam |
| 19 | PDB Structure | PDB |
| 20–21 | KEGG ID and pathways | KEGG |
| 22–24 | GO (F, C, P) | Gene Ontology |

Cells with no data display **`No data found`**.

## Cache and parallelism

### Cache

- File: `.cache/gene_annotations.json`
- Key: `GENE|organism` (normalized)
- Thread-safe for parallel mode
- Invalidate with `--clear-cache` or manual deletion

### Parallelism

- Up to **4 workers** (`--workers`, `MAX_WORKERS`)
- Each worker processes one gene independently
- Cache avoids redundant requests across sessions

::: {.callout-warning}
## API rate limits
NCBI recommends ≤ 3 requests/second without an API key. With `--workers 2` or more, watch for errors and increase `NCBI_DELAY` if needed.
:::

## Coverage report

`summary.py` computes for each column:

- Filled cells / total;
- Coverage percentage;
- Number of "complete" genes (all columns filled);
- Duration, cache vs fetch counts, and failure list.

This report is embedded in HTML, written to `*_summary.txt`, and included in JSON/manifest exports.

## Error handling

- Gene-level failure: the row is kept with `(ERROR: message)` in the first column;
- Other genes continue to be processed;
- Failures appear in the final report.

## Version

Current version: **1.1.0** (defined in `annotation/config.py`).
